title: Nutanix CVM Resource and Service Health
spl:
index=hci sourcetype="nutanix:cvm:metrics" earliest=-4h
| where cpu_pct>85 OR mem_pct>90 OR stargate_latency_ms>5
| stats max(cpu_pct) as max_cpu, max(mem_pct) as max_mem, max(stargate_latency_ms) as max_lat by node
| sort -max_lat
description: Deep-dive CVM CPU/memory pressure and Stargate latency vs UC-19.2.7 ‚Äî for capacity and noisy-neighbor triage.
value: Deep-dive CVM CPU/memory pressure and Stargate latency vs UC-19.2.7 ‚Äî for capacity and noisy-neighbor triage.

detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: `TA-nutanix`, Prism metrics.
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `nutanix:cvm:metrics`.
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
Poll Prism per-CVM metrics API. Alert on sustained high latency with high CPU (investigate disk/network). Correlate with storage rebuilds.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=hci sourcetype="nutanix:cvm:metrics" earliest=-4h
| where cpu_pct>85 OR mem_pct>90 OR stargate_latency_ms>5
| stats max(cpu_pct) as max_cpu, max(mem_pct) as max_mem, max(stargate_latency_ms) as max_lat by node
| sort -max_lat
```

Understanding this SPL

**Nutanix CVM Resource and Service Health** ‚Äî Deep-dive CVM CPU/memory pressure and Stargate latency vs UC-19.2.7 ‚Äî for capacity and noisy-neighbor triage.

Documented **Data sources**: `nutanix:cvm:metrics`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `TA-nutanix`, Prism metrics. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: hci; **sourcetype**: nutanix:cvm:metrics. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=hci, sourcetype="nutanix:cvm:metrics", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where cpu_pct>85 OR mem_pct>90 OR stargate_latency_ms>5` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by node** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (hot CVMs), Line chart (latency vs CPU), Heatmap (node √ó time).
grandmaExplanation: Deep-dive CVM CPU/memory pressure and Stargate latency vs UC-19.2.7 ‚Äî for capacity and noisy-neighbor triage ‚Äî so hardware faults and capacity problems do not take services down.
